levels.4: Expert-level reasoning with multi-step logic chains, considers edge cases and nuances, demonstrates deep domain understanding
levels.3: Solid reasoning that addresses the core problem correctly, minor gaps in explanation depth
levels.2: Surface-level explanation that identifies the right concept but lacks detailed reasoning
levels.1: Incorrect reasoning, missing key logical steps, or no reasoning provided

breadth.description: Coverage of relevant aspects and edge cases

levels.4: Covers all relevant aspects including edge cases, alternative approaches, and practical implications
levels.3: Covers main aspects of the topic with minor omissions
levels.2: Partial coverage, missing important aspects or perspectives
levels.1: Misses key aspects, addresses only a narrow slice of the question

accuracy.description: Factual correctness and absence of hallucinations

levels.4: Entirely factually correct, no hallucinations, all claims verifiable
levels.3: Minor inaccuracies that don't affect overall correctness, no hallucinations
levels.2: Some factual errors present, possible hallucinated details
levels.1: Major factual errors, clear hallucinations, or fundamentally wrong information

levels.4: Clear, well-structured, uses domain-appropriate terminology, good flow between ideas
levels.3: Clear with minor style issues, generally well-organized
levels.2: Understandable but poorly organized, inconsistent terminology or register
levels.1: Unclear, poorly structured, inappropriate register for the domain
